mini-project.label: Mini-Project: End-to-End Query
mini-project.summary: Combine your SQL skills to answer real business questions using the inventory_ops dataset.

intro-overview.title: Project Overview
intro-overview.bodyMarkdown: In this mini-project, you'll use the `inventory_items` table from the `inventory_ops` dataset. The table tracks products, their categories, prices, status, and whether they're test items. You'll write queries to answer practical business questions, such as identifying active products, calculating category totals, and filtering out test data.

table-schema.title: Table Schema & Sample Data
table-schema.bodyMarkdown:
The `inventory_items` table has these columns:
- `id` (INTEGER): Unique item ID
- `name` (TEXT): Product name
- `category` (TEXT): Product category
- `price` (REAL): Price in USD
- `status` (TEXT): Product status (`active`, `draft`, `inactive`)
- `is_test` (INTEGER): 1 if test item, 0 otherwise
- `notes` (TEXT): Optional notes
- `last_updated` (TEXT): Last update date

**Sample rows:**
| id | name          | category    | price | status   | is_test | notes             | last_updated |
|----|--------------|-------------|-------|----------|---------|-------------------|-------------|
| 1  | Sticker Pack | Accessories | 4.99  | active   | 0       | NULL              | 2026-02-01  |
| 2  | Test Mug     | Kitchen     | 7.50  | draft    | 1       | Internal sample   | 2026-02-03  |
| 3  | Canvas Print | Decor       | 45.00 | active   | 0       | NULL              | 2026-02-02  |

business-questions.title: Business Questions
business-questions.bodyMarkdown:
You'll answer questions such as:
- Which products are currently active and not test items?
- What is the total value of active inventory by category?
- Which items were last updated most recently?

These queries help businesses understand their real, sellable inventory and spot trends or issues.

identify-active-non-test.title: Identify Active, Non-Test Items
identify-active-non-test.prompt: Which SQL WHERE clause correctly selects only active products that are not test items from `inventory_items`?
identify-active-non-test.hint: Focus on both the status and the is_test flag.

help.concept: Filtering with WHERE lets you select rows matching multiple conditions using AND.
help.hint_1: You want only rows where status is 'active' and is_test is 0.
help.hint_2: Combine both conditions with AND to exclude test items and include only active ones.

category-total-value.title: Total Value by Category
category-total-value.prompt: Write a SQL query to show each product category and the total price of all active, non-test items in that category. The result should have columns `category` and `total_value`.
category-total-value.hint: Use SUM and GROUP BY for category totals.

help.concept: Aggregate functions like SUM can total values per group, and GROUP BY splits results by a column.
help.hint_1: Filter for active, non-test items before grouping.
help.hint_2: Group by category and sum the price for each group.

recently-updated-items.title: Order Items by Last Updated
recently-updated-items.prompt: Arrange the following SQL clauses to list all non-test items ordered from most recently updated to least recently updated.
recently-updated-items.hint: Start with SELECT and end with ORDER BY.

help.concept: SQL queries follow a specific order: SELECT, FROM, WHERE, then ORDER BY.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY DESC sorts from newest to oldest.

fill-blank-category.title: Fill in the Category Filter
fill-blank-category.prompt: Complete the SQL WHERE clause to select only items in the 'Decor' category that are not test items.
fill-blank-category.hint: Look for the correct category name.

help.concept: String values in SQL must match the column's data exactly.
help.hint_1: Check the sample data for the correct category spelling.
help.hint_2: Use the category name as shown in the table, with quotes.

multi-choice-status.title: Select All Valid Status Values
multi-choice-status.prompt: Which of the following are valid values for the `status` column in `inventory_items`? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-status.hint: Check the sample data for possible status values.

help.concept: The status column uses the correct answer fixed set of values as shown in the data.
help.hint_1: Look for all unique status values present in the sample rows.
help.hint_2: Ignore any value not found in the table's sample data.
